layout_description:
Option: OPT-1
Slide description: TWO-COLUMN LAYOUT with equal-width side-by-side tables spanning the full available slide space below the title. The slide uses a 4-column × 6-row grid structure with 3% safe-area margins on all sides and 2% horizontal gutter between the two table containers. Both tables are aligned at the top edge and share identical height, filling the vertical space from below the title to the bottom safe-area margin. The layout follows a left-to-right reading flow with the Bookings table on the left and the Revenues table on the right. No outer content container is used — the tables sit directly on the slide background. No decorative bottom layer is needed as the two tables provide full coverage of the content zone. The design applies Section 4 Option C (Flat Geometry Table) from the design guidelines: no filled header bars, accent tabs beneath column headers, light grey label cells for region/customer identifiers, and thin dashed grey separator lines between data rows. The visual treatment is formal and regulatory-appropriate, matching the financial data content register.

Blueprint:
  Orientation: left-to-right
  Regions: 2
  Allocation: [0.46, 0.54]

Containers:
  C0 (LEFT COLUMN — Bookings Table): Left-aligned table occupying the left half of the slide (split 1:1 with C1). Top edge aligns with C1 at approximately 13.5% from slide top. Height extends to fill remaining vertical space down to the bottom safe-area margin. Width is approximately 39% of slide width. Content: multi-column financial table showing Bookings data for 2026 by Region, Customer, Country, and quarterly breakdown (Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar). The table has approximately 18 rows including section headers and subtotals. Visual treatment follows Section 4 Option C (Flat Geometry Table): Column headers are plain bold text in brand dark color, left-aligned. Directly beneath each header label sits a small accent tab (brand primary color, very thin height, narrow width relative to column, top corners slightly rounded, bottom edge square, left-aligned with header text). Immediately below and touching each accent tab is a thin solid horizontal line in the same brand color, spanning that column's width with small white gutters between columns (not a single full-width line). The first column (Region) uses light grey filled rectangles (no border, no rounded corners, full column width, most of row height) with bold brand dark text left-aligned with a small left inset — these are the region label cells (NA, Intl). Customer and Country columns use plain text on white background, left-aligned. Numeric columns (Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar) use plain text on white, right-aligned for numeric values. Data rows are distributed evenly across the remaining vertical space below the header structure. Between each data row: thin dashed grey horizontal separator lines spanning the full table width. Subtotal rows (Total NA, Total Intl, Total) use bold text to distinguish them from data rows. Highlighted cells (visible green fills in source) preserve their exact highlight color as accent fills behind the cell text. All text uses the theme body font. Column headers are bold, slightly larger or same size as body. Data text is regular weight, standard body size. Font size is reduced proportionally to fit all 18 rows within the available container height without overflow — target range is medium body size, ensuring all numeric values and text labels fit comfortably within their cells. Column widths are content-weighted: Region and Customer columns sized to fit longest text without wrapping; Country column slightly narrower; numeric columns (Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar) share remaining width equally and are narrow enough to feel compact but wide enough to display 4-digit numbers comfortably.
  C1 (RIGHT COLUMN — Revenues Table): Right-aligned table occupying the right half of the slide (split 1:1 with C0). Top edge aligns exactly with C0 at approximately 13.5% from slide top. Height matches C0 exactly, extending to the bottom safe-area margin. Width is approximately 46% of slide width. Content: multi-column financial table showing Revenues data for 2026 by Region, Customer, Country, and quarterly breakdown (Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar). The table has approximately 18 rows including section headers and subtotals, mirroring the structure of C0. Visual treatment follows Section 4 Option C (Flat Geometry Table), identical to C0: Column headers are plain bold text in brand dark color, left-aligned. Directly beneath each header label sits a small accent tab (brand primary color, very thin height, narrow width, top corners slightly rounded, bottom edge square, left-aligned with header text). Immediately below and touching each accent tab is a thin solid horizontal line in the same brand color, spanning that column's width with small white gutters between columns. The first column (Region) uses light grey filled rectangles (no border, no rounded corners, full column width, most of row height) with bold brand dark text left-aligned with a small left inset. Customer and Country columns use plain text on white background, left-aligned. Numeric columns (Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar) use plain text on white, right-aligned for numeric values. Data rows are distributed evenly across the remaining vertical space below the header structure. Between each data row: thin dashed grey horizontal separator lines spanning the full table width. Subtotal rows (Total NA, Total Intl, Total) use bold text. Highlighted cells (visible green fills in source) preserve their exact highlight color as accent fills behind the cell text. All text uses the theme body font. Column headers are bold, slightly larger or same size as body. Data text is regular weight, standard body size. Font size matches C0 exactly — same reduction applied to maintain within-slide uniformity (Rule C6). Target range is medium body size, ensuring all numeric values and text labels fit comfortably within their cells without overflow. Column widths are content-weighted: Region and Customer columns sized to fit longest text without wrapping; Country column slightly narrower; numeric columns (Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar) share remaining width equally and are narrow enough to feel compact but wide enough to display 4-digit numbers comfortably. The right edge of this table respects the 3% right safe-area margin.
  SC-BOTTOM (DECORATIVE BOTTOM LAYER — Inactive): Not active. The two tables (C0 and C1) provide full coverage of the content zone with no significant empty space remaining. No visual grouping benefit would be added by a bottom decorative layer, and the formal financial data register does not call for additional background fills. The slide background remains white.
  SC-TOP (TOP CONNECTOR LAYER — Inactive): Not active. No cross-container connectors, arrows, or overlay elements are needed. The two tables are independent data presentations with no visual links between them.

_reference_violations.description: C1 (Revenues table): Column/row transposition detected. In the source slide, the Revenues table shows 'U Chicago Thundercat (VA)' as a single customer entry in one row, with 'InterMountain (IM)' as a separate customer entry in another row. In the sketch, these appear to be split incorrectly: 'U Chicago' appears in one row, 'Thundercat (VA)' in another row, and 'InterMountain (IM)' in a third row. This is a structural error where multi-line customer names have been incorrectly parsed as separate rows, altering the table structure.

C0 (Bookings table): Row structure error. The source slide shows 'JGH New US - 2026' and 'New CA - 2026' as two separate customer entries (two rows). In the sketch, these appear as 'JGH', 'New US - 2026', and 'New CA - 2026' in three separate rows, suggesting incorrect row parsing. This alters the table structure and row count.

C0 (Bookings table): Row structure error. The source slide shows 'Sheba Albert Einstein New Intl - 2026' as customer entries. In the sketch, 'Sheba Albert' appears as one entry and 'Albert Einstein' as another, suggesting incorrect parsing of customer names across rows. This is a row transposition/structure error.

C1 (Revenues table): The 'Sheba' row in the Intl section is missing from the sketch. The source slide clearly shows a 'Sheba' entry with values (118, 104, 7, 7) in the Revenues table, but this row does not appear in the sketch's C1 table.

_reference_critique:
CRITICAL content fidelity violations detected in both tables due to incorrect row parsing and customer name handling:

C0 (Bookings table):
1. Row structure error: 'JGH New US - 2026' and 'New CA - 2026' are rendered as three separate rows ('JGH', 'New US - 2026 US', 'New CA - 2026 CA') instead of two rows. The source slide shows these as two distinct customer entries under the NA region. SVGSketcher must parse the source data correctly: 'JGH' should be combined with 'New US - 2026' as one customer entry, and 'New CA - 2026' should be a separate customer entry.

2. Row structure error: 'Sheba Albert' and 'Albert Einstein' appear to be incorrectly split. The source shows 'Sheba', 'Albert Einstein', and 'New Intl - 2026' as three separate customer rows under the Intl region. SVGSketcher must verify the exact customer names from the source and render each as a distinct row without merging or splitting names incorrectly.

C1 (Revenues table):
1. CRITICAL row structure error: 'U Chicago Thundercat (VA)' and 'InterMountain (IM)' are multi-line customer names in the source that must each occupy a SINGLE row. In the sketch, these appear to be split across multiple rows ('U Chicago', 'Thundercat (VA)', 'InterMountain (IM)' as three separate rows). SVGSketcher must treat each complete customer name (including parenthetical suffixes) as a single table row, preserving the exact row structure from the source.

2. Missing row: The 'Sheba' customer row (with values 118, 104, 7, 7) is completely absent from the Intl section of C1. SVGSketcher must include all customer rows from the source Revenues table.

SVGSketcher must re-extract the table data from the source slide with correct row boundaries, ensuring each customer entry occupies exactly one row and all rows are present. Pay special attention to multi-line customer names and ensure they are not split into separate rows.
